body_txt: Take real-life events, fairy tales that have expanded on each telling and doorstopping epics and consider all the details and nuance that contributes to the final result. Then strip away all the complexities: characters motivations are reduced to black and white moralizing , generations of events simplified to one solitary lynchpin and a dramatic, weeks long conflict is altered to one Curb-Stomp Battle . At its worst, this new version of events involves a misunderstanding of what actually happened, believing it to be something entirely contrary to what it meant or piggybacking on others interpretations . That is the Theme Park Version, altering the details and removing the nuance to the point where anyone who is introduced to it this way already has a fundamental misunderstanding of the subject. Thus, someone who knows the original and someone who knows the recent adaptation will have very little to talk about. Another word for this concept is a "simulacrum". While in general this should be avoided, it's not always a bad thing . You put up enough signposts and people can still get the cliff notes version of what happened, allowing something to be correct even though it goes on to use and abuse the Rule of Drama or Rule of Funny to tell an entertaining story. While dumbing down the material means that it isn't as complex or interesting, it also trims out a lot of exposition , making the media more accessible and/or less restricted. Sometimes, this oversimplification is necessary to make it a compelling experience that fits in a single work - try to fit the aforementioned dramatic weeks-long conflict with all its nuance inside a game marketed as a fun pick-up-and-play multiplayer experience, and it'll most likely be accused of indulging in Padding or Misaimed "Realism" . See also Pragmatic Adaptation , when an adaptation has to leave some concepts on the cutting room floor for the sake of expediency, or Artistic License , when creators play fast and loose with real-life facts because it'll make for a better experience. Compare with Adaptation Decay , Adaptation Distillation , In Name Only , Flanderization , Lost in Imitation , Small Reference Pools , Cowboy BeBop at His Computer , Pop-Cultural Osmosis . Theme parks themselves have their own Theme Park Version — Souvenir Land . Mildly Military commonly results when this is applied to stories set in war or otherwise military environments. See also: Flynning , Fluffy Cloud Heaven , Fire and Brimstone Hell , Pac-Man Fever , Plot Tumor and Hollywood Atlas . If you're concerned about avoiding falling into this, give this page a read.
